global_rules: CRITICAL: The answer is NEVER encoded in the visual. Visuals show setup only.
AP exam tests all four representations: graphical, numerical, analytical, verbal. Visuals primarily serve graphical and numerical representations.
Coordinate planes use clean grid with labeled axes. Tick marks every 1 unit unless otherwise noted.
All curves drawn with smooth, mathematically accurate shape — not schematic.
Dashed lines for asymptotes; solid dots for defined points; open dots for excluded points (holes).
All visuals must be renderable in SVG format for web delivery.
Color convention: black for curve, blue for labeled values, red for unknowns/questions, gray for asymptotes.

visual_specs.title: Covariation — Graph with Intervals Marked
visual_specs.description: A generic function graph with several intervals marked on the x-axis. Intervals where f is increasing, decreasing, or constant are visible but NOT labeled. Student must identify behavior.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Over which interval is f increasing?

visual_specs.title: Average Rate of Change — Secant Line Setup
visual_specs.description: A curve with two points marked at x=a and x=b. The secant line is drawn through these points but the slope value is NOT labeled.
visual_specs.format_hint: Gridded: Compute the average rate of change from x=a to x=b.

visual_specs.title: Polynomial Graph — Zero Behavior
visual_specs.description: Polynomial graph shown. Zeros visible as x-intercepts. Graph shows crosses at some zeros and bounces at others — but multiplicities are NOT labeled. Student reads behavior.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Which polynomial equation could match this graph?

visual_specs.title: Rational Function — Asymptotes Without Labels
visual_specs.description: Rational function graphed with visible horizontal and vertical asymptotes (dashed gray lines). Asymptote values NOT labeled — only positions visible.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC or expression: Identify the equations of all asymptotes.

visual_specs.title: Rational Function — Hole Visible, Not Labeled
visual_specs.description: Simplified rational function shown with one open circle indicating a hole. The hole coordinates are NOT labeled.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Find the exact coordinates of the hole.

visual_specs.title: Function Transformation — Original and Transformed
visual_specs.description: Parent function f (solid) and a transformed function g (dashed) shown on the same plane. Labels say 'f' and 'g' only — no equation of g shown.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Which equation represents g in terms of f?

visual_specs.title: Scatter Plot for Model Selection
visual_specs.description: Scatter plot with data points showing a specific pattern (rapid growth then leveling, or clear parabolic shape, etc.). No regression line shown — student selects appropriate model.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Which function type best models this data? Justify.

visual_specs.title: Exponential Function — Graph with Two Points
visual_specs.description: Exponential curve with two labeled points. The equation is NOT shown. Student determines a and b from the points.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Find f(x) = a·b^x.

visual_specs.title: Composition Input/Output — f and g Graphs
visual_specs.description: Two separate graphs side by side: f(x) on left, g(x) on right. Specific x-values are labeled on g's graph. Student reads g(x) then finds f(g(x)).
visual_specs.format_hint: Numeric: Find f(g(2)) using the graphs.

visual_specs.title: Inverse Functions — y=x Reflection
visual_specs.description: f(x) graphed (solid) and its inverse f⁻¹(x) graphed (dashed). Line y=x shown in gray. No equations labeled — student must identify which is f and which is f⁻¹.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC or expression: Identify which curve is f⁻¹ and write its equation.

visual_specs.title: Logarithmic Function — Transformed
visual_specs.description: Log function graph with visible vertical asymptote (dashed) and labeled points (key points like x-intercept and one other). Equation NOT shown.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Write the equation of this function.

visual_specs.title: Semi-Log Plot — log(y) vs x
visual_specs.description: Scatter plot with y-axis labeled as log(y) or ln(y). Data falls approximately linearly. The regression line is shown. Slope and intercept NOT labeled.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Determine the exponential model y = f(x) from the semi-log plot.

visual_specs.title: Periodic Phenomenon — Real World Context
visual_specs.description: Sinusoidal-looking curve over time, labeled with context (height, temperature, etc.). Max and min points are visible but NOT labeled with coordinates. Student extracts features.
visual_specs.format_hint: Numeric: Identify amplitude, period, midline from the graph.

visual_specs.title: Unit Circle — Angle and Coordinates
visual_specs.description: Unit circle with ONE specific angle shown (terminal side drawn). The coordinates of the terminal point are NOT labeled. Student finds exact trig values.
visual_specs.format_hint: Exact: Find sin(θ), cos(θ), tan(θ).

visual_specs.title: Sine/Cosine Graph — Read Parameters
visual_specs.description: Full sinusoidal graph (2 full periods). Midline shown as dashed. Max and min visible. NO equation shown. Student identifies A, B, D.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Write f(x) = Asin(Bx + C) + D or Acos(Bx + C) + D.

visual_specs.title: Sinusoidal Model — Context with Labeled Min/Max Time
visual_specs.description: Graph showing periodic context (e.g., tide height). Max height time and min height time are labeled on x-axis. Heights NOT labeled as y-values. Only context info given in problem statement.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Write f(t) = Asin(B(t-h)) + D using context.

visual_specs.title: Sinusoidal Concavity — Mark the Interval
visual_specs.description: Sine curve drawn over two periods. No shading. Student must identify concave up and concave down intervals from the curve's shape.
visual_specs.frq_alignment: FRQ 3: Modeling Periodic Context Part C
visual_specs.format_hint: Interval notation: On which intervals is f concave up?

visual_specs.title: Polar Plane — Plot Point
visual_specs.description: Polar coordinate plane with circles (r=1,2,3,4) and angle lines (every π/6). ONE point marked — student identifies or converts coordinates.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Give the rectangular coordinates of the marked point.

visual_specs.title: Polar Curve — Identify the Type
visual_specs.description: A polar curve plotted on a polar plane. The shape is shown (rose, cardioid, limaçon, circle) but the equation is NOT labeled. Student identifies the curve type or matches equation.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Which equation matches this polar curve?

visual_specs.title: Parametric Curve — Plot Only
visual_specs.description: Parametric curve plotted (ellipse, parabola, or line segment). Direction arrow shown on curve. Equation NOT shown. Student eliminates parameter from context clues or given equations.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Eliminate parameter to write y as function of x.

visual_specs.title: Conic Section — Identify and Label
visual_specs.description: Conic section shown on coordinate plane. Center, vertices visible but NOT labeled. Axes of symmetry shown as dashed.
visual_specs.format_hint: Expression: Write the standard form equation of this conic.

visual_specs.title: End Behavior — Graph Showing Both Ends
visual_specs.description: Polynomial graph shown with clear behavior at both ends. The arrows at each end are visible. Degree and leading coefficient NOT labeled.
visual_specs.format_hint: Using limit notation: Describe end behavior as x→+∞ and x→-∞.

visual_specs.title: Exponential Decay vs Growth — Two Curves
visual_specs.description: Two exponential curves on the same axes — one growth, one decay. NO equations labeled. Student identifies which is which and reads key values.
visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Which equation could be Curve B? Justify.

visual_specs.title: Unit Circle — All Four Quadrants
visual_specs.description: Full unit circle with angles labeled at standard positions (multiples of π/6 and π/4). Coordinates are NOT shown — student must recall from memory.
visual_specs.format_hint: Exact: Fill in sin(θ) and cos(θ) for the indicated angle.

visual_specs.title: Polynomial — From Factored Form Setup
visual_specs.description: BLANK graph with only axes shown and marked zeros on x-axis. The curve itself is NOT drawn. Student uses zeros and end behavior to sketch or match.
visual_specs.format_hint: Graph-match: Which graph matches the polynomial with these zeros and this leading coefficient?

visual_specs.title: Function Family Comparison — Exp vs Log
visual_specs.description: Two curves on same axes: f(x) = bˣ and its inverse g(x) = log_b(x). Line y=x shown. Student identifies which is which and answers questions about domain/range.

elements.labels: f and g only — no equations

visual_specs.format_hint: MC: Which is the exponential function? What is the domain of g?

visual_specs.title: FRQ 3 Style — Five Key Points Template
visual_specs.description: Blank sinusoidal framework showing the midline and one period boundary. Student must plot five key points (max, min, midline crossings) based on calculated A, B, C, D.
visual_specs.frq_alignment: FRQ 3: Modeling Periodic Context Part A
visual_specs.format_hint: Graphing: Plot five labeled points and sketch two full cycles.

visual_specs.title: ARC — Secant Line on Exponential
visual_specs.description: Exponential curve with two points marked (t₁, P₁) and (t₂, P₂). Secant line drawn. Units labeled on axes. ARC value NOT shown.
visual_specs.format_hint: Numeric with units: Calculate the ARC and interpret in context.
